findings.problem: The search middle repeats the same functional beat too many times: Lucien and Elise ask another colorful walk-on about Abel, receive another south-pointing clue, and move on. The ordinary-city texture is strong, but by the time Borel names the tollhouse the chapter has already burned a lot of momentum on serial confirmation rather than escalation, obstacle, or cost.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or differentiate the inquiry chain at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:41, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:75. Keep the urban-life pressure, but make at least one stop add a concrete obstacle, false lead, refusal, or interpersonal cost instead of another version of "south," so Borel's tollhouse clue feels like a turn rather than a fourth confirmation.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, reading the revised search run anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:41, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:75, no more than two same-function inquiry beats occur in sequence and at least one encounter materially changes leverage before the tollhouse is identified. Fail if each stop still mainly restates that Abel or Ferrand were heading south.

findings.problem: The chapter leans on the same transition scaffold six times: "had started to" or "had begun to" does the work of registering change in mood, heat, light, and motion. At this frequency it stops reading as deliberate syntax and starts reading as an autopilot sentence shape, which is exactly the kind of recurring prose formula the constitution forbids.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the transition sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:85, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:173 so each shift is carried by a concrete physical change specific to that moment rather than the repeated "had started/begun to" construction.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:85, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:173, at least four of the six targeted sentences no longer use "had started to" or "had begun to" and each one conveys a distinct sensory shift when read in context. Fail if this formula still appears more than twice across those targeted transitions or the revised lines remain interchangeable in rhythm and function.

summary: The chapter clears its causal obligations and its voice obligations: the ordinary-city search, Lucien's apricot purchase, and the wall-side admission all land, dialogue stays distinct and appropriately contracted, and I found no continuity contradiction or narrative-tense drift against the supplied packs. It still fails review on craft and prose because the first-half search chain repeats too many same-function clue beats before the tollhouse turn, and the narration falls into a noticeable "had started to / had begun to" transition tic that blunts urgency.
